items.title: Bahrain, Kuwait targeted by fresh Iran salvoes
items.summary: Bahrain, home to the headquarters of the U.S. Fifth Fleet, denounced the attacks against its territory and neighbouring Kuwait

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: Bahrain says targeted by Iran, denounces ‘blatant aggression’

items.title: More than 1,000 Indians deported from U.S. in 2026
items.summary: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said a total 3,567 Indians were deported from U.S. to India in 2025

items.title: British PM Starmer criticises U.S. V-P Vance over comments on U.K. teen's death
items.summary: J.D. Vance wrote on X that there should be “righteous anger” in response to the murder of Henry Nowak, which he blamed in part on “the mass invasion of migrants, many of whom despise the West and the people who love it”

items.title: Putin says he sees “no point” in personal meeting with Zelensky
North Korean leader Kim showcases new warship ahead of visit by China’s Xi
Iran dismisses idea of Trump meeting Supreme Leader
items.summary: Iran's top diplomat however played down the chances in an interview with Lebanese media aired on Thursday (June 4, 2026) night, suggesting it was not realistic

items.title: Millions are being pushed into hunger by Iran war: UN food agency
Lebanon considers largest amnesty in 35 years amid protests
items.summary: Lebanon's largest amnesty since the end of its devastating 1975-90 civil war is expected to occur in the coming weeks, once Parliament approves it and the President signs it

items.title: Argentina expands hantavirus probe; sends teams to test rats in Mendoza
items.summary: The Health Ministry says specialists from Malbran, together with U.S. CDC, are preparing to test rodents for the hantavirus in Mendoza from June 8-12

items.title: ​Incomplete truce: On the  Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
items.summary: Israel has to pull back troops from Lebanon for peace to take hold

items.title: Did Meta’s own AI help hack into Instagram users’ accounts? | Explained
items.summary: A reportedly AI-enabled hacking incident that impacted Instagram account users was further complicated by a complete lack of clarity and transparency on Meta’s end

items.title: North Korean leader Kim showcases new warship ahead of visit by China’s Xi
items.summary: North Korea’s official Korean Central News Agency said Kim visited the 5,000-tonne destroyer Kang Kon as it underwent capability tests

items.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump administration immigration policy affecting 39 countries
items.summary: U.S. District Chief Judge said the policy “threw the lives of countless immigrants living in the United States into indeterminate legal limbo”, and he accused the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services of ignoring the law

items.title: Trump says Iran has ‘22%’ of missiles left
items.summary: That figure for Iran's missile stockpile is higher than one of 18% Mr. Trump gave in May

items.title: Putin rules out meeting Zelenskyy and vows to pursue war goals
items.summary: The Russia President said he saw “no point” in meeting Mr. Zelenskyy until a possible peace deal had been agreed; the Ukrainian President hit back, saying the Russian leader was “weak” and “choosing war again”

items.title: U.S. military says it shot down Iranian missiles, drones launched toward Gulf allies, Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict

items.title: Court can't stop Trump ballroom construction, government lawyer tells judge
items.summary: Attorney Yaakov Roth, speaking during an exchange with U.S. Appeals Court Judge Patricia Millett, said only Congress could halt the $400 million project.

items.title: ‘Albania is not for sale’, protesters say over Kushner-linked luxury resort near a protected wetland
items.summary: Environmentalists ‌oppose the project, which they say would affect several hundred hectares of ‌pristine ⁠beaches and thousands of flamingos that nest and pass through nearby areas each ⁠year.

items.title: Israel-Iran war highlights: U.S. strikes Iran radar sites after Iranian drone launch
items.summary: The U.S. military shot down ‌all of ​the Iranian drones, saying they “posed ⁠an immediate threat to ‌regional maritime traffic.”

items.title: West Asia war highlights: Trump slams 'unpatriotic' U.S. House vote to end Iran war
items.summary: Hezbollah rejects the latest ceasefire agreement as Israeli strikes kill four in Lebanon; demands 'comprehensive' ceasefire, Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon

items.title: Watch: Cabinet Trouble for D.K. Shivakumar as Ramalinga Reddy Resigns | Above the Fold | 05.06.2026
items.summary: In today’s episode, we look at Karnataka Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar’s first political crisis, as Ramalinga Reddy resigns from Cabinet. We also ask our reporters to break down what K. Annamalai’s latest announcement means for Tamil Nadu politics.

items.title: UN doubles appeal for Lebanon aid to nearly $640 mn amid Israel war
items.summary: Three months into the war, the United Nations warned that a "humanitarian catastrophe" was looming in Lebanon, with a quarter of its population needing aid

items.title: Palestinians call on Trump to stop Israeli annexation
items.summary: The UN's geopolitical bloc Arab Group joined the ambassador to express "deep alarm" and regional solidarity against "rapidly escalating" Israeli settler aggression and military violence against Palestinians

items.title: Russian strikes kill 7 in Ukraine, destroy children's food plant
items.summary: "The enemy attacked a peaceful civilian food industry enterprise," Kyiv region governor Mykola Kalashnyk said in a post on Telegram

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon after evacuation warnings to several villages
items.summary: Israel’s air force has struck parts of southern Lebanon, and the military issued evacuation warnings for nine villages

items.title: Pressure on Modi to limit India-Russia ties will hurt global stability: Putin
items.summary: A “large economy” like India will prioritise national interests, and its relations with the U.S. will not cause friction with Russia, says Vladimir Putin; Russian President says it is “not a good idea” to interfere in the “delicate” India-China ties

items.title: WHO announces $518 million six-month plan to fight Ebola
items.summary: So far there have been 381 confirmed cases ‌in Congo and 62 confirmed deaths, according ​to Africa CDC.

items.title: NASA orders astronauts to take shelter after new leak aboard the International Space Station
items.summary: The decision was made “out of an abundance of caution,” NASA spokesperson said

items.title: 13 foreigners among dead in Delhi hotel fire, in touch with embassies: MEA
items.summary: About 20-22 foreign nationals have sustained injuries in the fire incident, according to MEA.

items.title: Watch: Trump: ‘95% of Cubans voted for me, I want to take good care of them’
Five Azerbaijanis killed in Azov Sea drone attack: Baku
items.summary: Five Azerbaijani nationals were killed and three others injured in a drone attack on two foreign cargo ships in the Sea of Azov, according to Azerbaijan’s Foreign Ministry

items.title: Former Tibet government head sentenced to life in prison
items.summary: Che Dalha illegally took in more than 158 million yuan ($23.35 million) from 1999 to ‌2025 while he worked in the country's southwestern border regions of Tibet and ‌Yunnan, as well as during postings in ‌Beijing, a court said

items.title: Thailand to appoint conciliators for UN-backed mediation with Cambodia, says Foreign Minister
items.summary: Cambodia said ​it had launched a compulsory conciliation ⁠process under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea

items.title: Former Prince Andrew made money subletting cottages on his rent-free estate, report shows
items.summary: It also disclosed that his daughters, Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie, live in rent-controlled palace properties paid for by their uncle, King Charles III

items.title: Peru Presidential candidates make final pitch in tight, crime-driven race
items.summary: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori and leftist rival Roberto Sanchez made their final appeals to voters ahead of Presidential runoff election on June 7

items.title: Seven killed as Israel strikes in south Lebanon’s Tyre: civil defence
items.summary: Israeli military issued new evacuation warnings for nine southern Lebanese towns and villages, with Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reporting people were fleeing

items.title: Israeli fire kills at least 18 in Gaza, U.S. envoy visits hostage family protest
items.summary: The danger facing aid seekers in Gaza has compounded what international hunger experts this week called a “worst-case scenario of famine” in the besieged enclave.

items.title: First privately developed U.S. nuclear reactor in over 40 years achieves first criticality point
items.summary: The Department of Energy described the milestone, achieved on June 4, as "rebirth of America’s nuclear industry" and a "historic moment" for U.S. energy

items.title: Watch: Is the Israel–U.S. alliance straining over Iran?
items.summary: Tensions appear to be emerging within the Israel–U.S. alliance as differences grow over how to deal with Iran. While Washington seems increasingly open to diplomatic engagement and a potential agreement, Israel continues to advocate a tougher, security-first approach amid escalating regional tensions.In this video, we examine the latest developments in Lebanon, President Trump’s dilemma, the UAE’s role in regional diplomacy, and more.

items.title: Senate passes $70 billion immigration enforcement bill without limits on Trump settlement fund
items.summary: Senators voted 52-47 for the $70 billion legislation to fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol for the next three years, through the end of Mr. Trump's term

items.title: Watch: U.S.- Iran war: Peace talks stall after Hezbollah rejects truce
items.summary: A proposed Lebanon-Israel truce faces uncertainty after Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected the conditional ceasefire announced by Lebanese and Israeli envoys. Hezbollah is demanding a comprehensive ceasefire and a full Israeli withdrawal, while warning of renewed attacks on northern Israel.

items.title: China ready to work with Myanmar to maintain border stability: Wang Yi
items.summary: “Beijing hopes Myanmar ​would ‌ensure the safety of Chinese personnel,” Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi tells his ‌Myanmar counterpart Tin Maung Swe

items.title: Pakistan ‘forever grateful’ to Trump for India ceasefire intervention: PM Sharif
items.summary: At an event marking the 250th anniversary of American independence, Shehbaz Sharif described Pakistan-U.S. ties as a "true and special relationship" spanning nearly eight decades

items.title: UN nuclear watchdog says it’s been unable to inspect Iranian facilities
items.summary: The  International Atomic Energy Agency warned that it was “unable to discharge its safeguards responsibilities” that it has under the Safeguards Agreement of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty

items.title: Gunmen kidnap seven students from school in northwestern Nigeria
items.summary: One student escaped and was in custody; the police said it wasn't clear where the students were taken but efforts were under way to rescue the remaining six

items.title: Brunei's sultan announces cabinet shake-up, appoints sons as Ministers
items.summary: In a televised ⁠broadcast, Sultan Hassanal said he would continue to hold the key roles of Prime Minister, Defence Minister, and Finance Minister

items.title: India condemns attack on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon; seeks accountability
items.summary: Sergeant Milovan Jovanovic, a Serbian peacekeeper serving with the UNIFIL, was killed when a mortar struck a UN position near Marji'yun on June 3

items.title: Indonesia volcano erupts, forcing airport to close
items.summary: The volcanology agency said residents near rivers should also remain on alert for hazardous floods of volcanic material, known as lahar, if heavy rain occurs

items.title: Chinese leader Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week in first visit in years
items.summary: The announcement came a day after North Korea unveiled a new facility to produce nuclear bomb fuels

items.title: U.K. fire crews battle blaze at Tata Steel's plant in Wales
items.summary: The company said emergency services remain on site and are working with local teams to completely “extinguish the fire”

items.title: Is the war in Ukraine escalating?
items.summary: Are civilian targets becoming central to battlefield strategy? Could the Ukraine war trigger a wider global crisis? Are external powers deepening their involvement in Ukraine? Is growing public discontent weakening support for the war in Russia?

items.title: U.S. House passes Bill to aid Ukraine and impose new sanctions on Russia
items.summary: The 226-195 vote is a sign of impatience with President Donald Trump’s approach to the war and represents the House’s second major foreign policy break with Mr. Trump this week

items.title: India among Russia's top foreign trade partners: PM Mishustin
items.summary: In the financial year 2024–25, total India-Russia trade reached an all-time high of about $68.7 billion, up sharply from roughly $13 billion in 2021, marking nearly a five - to six- fold increase over four years.

items.title: In open letter to Putin, Zelenskyy calls for meeting and ceasefire
items.summary: Ukraine proposes ending this war through direct engagement between us — and you. I am proposing a meeting, says Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy

items.title: U.S. imposes sanctions on Cuban President, Treasury website shows
items.summary: The sanctions were announced as U.S. President ‌Donald Trump told reporters that the U.S. wanted Cuba “to be a nicely run country.”

items.title: Trump announces $700 million funding for coal plants, energy infrastructure
items.summary: Today, we’re taking historic action to bring down the price of energy and the cost of living for all Americans, with the power of clean, beautiful coal, says U.S. President Donald Trump

items.title: How ISWAP emerged as the dominant jihadist force in Africa’s Lake Chad basin
items.summary: The Islamic State West Africa Province steadily eclipsed Boko Haram to become the most powerful militant organisation in the region; with a structured command system and strong territorial presence, what began as a local insurgency in Nigeria is now one of Africa’s major transnational security threats

items.title: Five Eyes alliance warns that China is targeting personnel via fake job ads
items.summary: The alliance said Chinese agents pretending to be human resources consultants for legitimate-looking companies use websites such as LinkedIn, Indeed and Upwork to post job ads

items.title: Sudan, DR Congo top world's most neglected crises: Norway aid group
items.summary: The NGO's list is based on three criteria: lack of humanitarian funding, lack of media coverage, and lack of political will within the international community
